hero.title: Farever
hero.description: Farever is an online multiplayer action RPG set in a vibrant fantasy open world. Explore Siagarta, clear dungeons, craft powerful gear, build your own class style, and follow the Early Access roadmap.

gameFeature.title: Farever Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore Siagarta, combine class skills with weapons, clear dungeons with friends, and shape your progression through jobs, crafting, and gear upgrades.

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Farever Early Access Release and Download
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Farever is live on Steam Early Access, and this section gives the fastest launch snapshot for new and returning players.

steps.title: Check the current release status
steps.description: The game is available now in Steam Early Access for PC, with active live-service updates during the access period.

steps.title: Review launch pricing by region
steps.description: Pricing varies by currency region. Check your Steam storefront to confirm the current local price and launch promotions.

steps.title: Confirm platform and account setup
steps.description: A Steam account and PC installation are required. Keep your client updated to avoid matchmaking and version mismatch issues.

steps.title: Pick the closest server region
steps.description: Choosing the nearest region reduces latency, improves combat responsiveness, and stabilizes party play in instanced content.

quickTips: Use the Steam store page as the source of truth for price and package updates.
If a launch offer is active, buy within the visible countdown window in your region.
Coordinate server region with friends before creating your first party session.
Keep graphics drivers current before first launch to avoid shader compilation issues.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Farever Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Early progress in Farever is smoother when you follow a simple loop: explore, fight, gather, craft, and then push into harder content.

cards.name: Start with map awareness
cards.description: Learn travel routes, safe recovery spots, and fast return paths before you chain difficult encounters.

cards.name: Commit to one class first
cards.description: Focus one class and one role in your first hours so your gear and skill decisions stay efficient.

cards.name: Rotate activities for materials
cards.description: Alternate open-world events, dungeon runs, and gathering routes to keep both combat and crafting progress moving.

cards.name: Upgrade in short cycles
cards.description: Apply small upgrades frequently instead of waiting for perfect drops. Consistent gains matter more than rare spikes.

milestones: Complete your first world activity chain
Craft your first meaningful gear upgrade

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Farever Classes Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Farever launches with four core classes. Choose based on role responsibility, positioning comfort, and the pace you enjoy in combat.

items.name: Warrior
items.description: Durable melee control with steady pressure. Great for players who want direct engagement and stable survivability.

items.name: Cleric
items.description: Team utility and sustain focus. Strong in group content where timing and support rotations shape fight consistency.

items.name: Mage
items.description: Spell-driven damage from range. Rewards clean spacing and cooldown sequencing for safe, high-impact output.

items.name: Rogue
items.description: Fast melee burst with high movement demands. Best for aggressive players who can manage risk windows.

items.name: Solo class priority
items.description: Warrior is the easiest starting point for solo progression due to forgiving frontline play and smooth early stability.

items.name: Party class priority
items.description: Cleric and Warrior form the most reliable beginner duo foundation before scaling into specialized team compositions.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Farever Best Class and Role Picks
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Pick classes by objective instead of chasing a universal tier list. The best option changes with your role and group context.

solutions.name: Best beginner baseline
solutions.description: Strong survivability and straightforward combat flow make Warrior the most stable first pick for new players.

solutions.name: Best co-op anchor
solutions.description: Cleric raises team consistency through sustain and support utility, especially in extended dungeon encounters.

solutions.name: Best ranged pressure
solutions.description: Mage excels when your team needs safe damage uptime and strong ranged burst during boss mechanics.

solutions.name: Best high-tempo melee
solutions.description: Rogue is ideal for players comfortable with mobility-intensive execution and aggressive timing windows.

managementTips: For solo starts, prioritize durability and recoverability before peak damage.
For duos, pair frontline stability with support coverage for smoother clears.
For full parties, diversify roles to avoid overlapping cooldown gaps.
Re-evaluate your role after each major gear milestone rather than every run.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Farever Builds and Playstyles
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Build strength in Farever comes from class identity, weapon synergy, skill sequencing, and stat discipline working together.

cards.name: Bruiser control builds
cards.description: Frontline setups that trade burst for uptime, using mitigation and sustained pressure to hold space in group fights.

cards.name: Ranged burst builds
cards.description: High-output casting patterns focused on cooldown timing, spacing, and safe windows during boss phases.

cards.name: Mobility pressure builds
cards.description: Fast melee patterns that chain movement skills into burst opportunities while avoiding overcommitment.

cards.name: Support utility builds
cards.description: Team-first configurations that stabilize encounters through sustain, mitigation, and utility skill cadence.

highlights: Match weapon families to your role goal
Stack stats that reinforce your combat loop
Use defensive tools before overloading damage slots
Refine one build path at a time for clear upgrades

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Farever Weapons and Arsenal Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Weapon choice changes how each class feels. Focus on handling profile, skill synergy, and encounter type when building an arsenal.

regions.name: Close-range weapon sets
regions.description: Best for sustained pressure and control in melee lanes. Pair with durability and recovery-focused stats.

regions.name: Ranged catalyst sets
regions.description: Supports spacing-heavy combat with strong burst windows and flexible utility between cooldown cycles.

regions.name: High-tempo duel sets
regions.description: Fast execution profile for players who prefer short commit windows and active positional control.

regions.name: Hybrid support sets
regions.description: Balanced loadouts that maintain utility uptime while preserving enough personal damage to contribute.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Farever Combat Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Winning harder encounters is less about raw stats and more about timing discipline, positioning, and coordinated cooldown use.

creatures.name: Open with spacing checks
creatures.description: Start each pull by reading enemy reach and movement speed before committing high-cooldown skills.

creatures.name: Dodge before burst
creatures.description: Avoid avoidable damage first, then convert recovery windows into safe burst cycles.

creatures.name: Preserve one emergency tool
creatures.description: Keep one defensive cooldown or mobility option for unexpected mechanics and late-phase pressure spikes.

creatures.name: Respect boss phase shifts
creatures.description: When phase behavior changes, reset spacing and re-establish role positioning before re-engaging.

creatures.name: Coordinate party roles
creatures.description: Call defensive rotations and target priority to avoid overlapping mistakes in multi-wave fights.

creatures.name: Track stamina and cooldown rhythm
creatures.description: Stable clears come from rhythm control, not constant aggression. Plan tempo around reliable resource windows.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Farever Map and Exploration Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Exploration efficiency in Siagarta depends on route planning, vertical traversal awareness, and consistent return loops for upgrades.

items.name: Primary region loops
items.description: Build repeatable loops between activities, gathering points, and recovery hubs to reduce idle travel time.

items.name: Landmark-based navigation
items.description: Use distinct terrain markers and settlement anchors to keep route memory reliable across long sessions.

items.name: Vertical pathing
items.description: Prioritize movement tools that shorten climbs and descents, especially near event-dense terrain.

items.name: Cave and side-path sweeps
items.description: Secondary paths frequently contain valuable materials and shortcut opportunities for future runs.

items.name: Underwater and hazard routing
items.description: Approach hazard zones with planned entry and exit points so failed attempts do not waste progression time.

items.name: Session-end consolidation
items.description: Finish each route near a crafting or storage hub to immediately convert gathered resources into progression.

unlockMilestones: Establish one fast-return route to core hubs
Integrate side-path farming into regular travel
End runs with direct upgrade conversion

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Farever Dungeons Instances and Bosses
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Dungeon progress is where role clarity and build readiness get tested. Enter with a plan for pacing, checkpoints, and boss mechanics.

sections.name: Pre-run readiness
sections.description: Validate potion stock, repair state, role assignments, and core cooldown coverage before starting high-value runs.

sections.name: Instance pacing
sections.description: Clear in controlled pulls instead of rushing packs. Consistent pacing prevents wipe cascades and preserves resources.

sections.name: Boss pattern adaptation
sections.description: Identify the first repeatable mechanic quickly, then assign movement priorities around that pattern.

sections.name: Reward conversion
sections.description: Immediately convert dungeon rewards into targeted upgrades so each run increases the next clear threshold.

growthMilestones: Finish first clean dungeon run with no wipe chain
Clear one boss with planned role rotations
Link dungeon drops to build upgrade priorities
Scale into harder instances with stable timing

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Farever Loot and Drop Tables
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Loot planning is faster when you separate mandatory upgrades from optional chase items and farm with clear targets.

priorities.name: Core progression materials
priorities.description: Prioritize materials that unblock immediate gear upgrades and class-defining craft steps.

priorities.name: Role-specific weapon drops
priorities.description: Farm weapons that directly reinforce your current build loop before branching into off-role options.

priorities.name: Boss utility drops
priorities.description: Target drops that solve survival or mobility bottlenecks in your next dungeon bracket.

priorities.name: Crafting conversion items
priorities.description: Collect components that turn common materials into high-value upgrades through your active job path.

priorities.name: Collection extras
priorities.description: Pursue cosmetic or long-tail collection pieces after your combat baseline is fully stable.

priorities.name: Market awareness picks
priorities.description: Track frequently requested drops so group farming sessions can deliver value for all party members.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Farever Crafting Jobs and Recipes
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Jobs convert gathered materials into progression power. Choose one main route early, then add secondary specializations.

achievements.title: Blacksmithing Foundations
achievements.description: Craft baseline gear upgrades for frontline and hybrid builds.

achievements.title: Cooking Utility
achievements.description: Prepare recovery and utility consumables for longer dungeon sessions.

achievements.title: Alchemy Prep
achievements.description: Build stock of combat consumables for boss attempts and route farming.

achievements.title: Common Material Loop
achievements.description: Create repeatable gathering routes for always-needed base components.

achievements.title: Rare Component Routing
achievements.description: Reserve rare drops for major upgrades instead of low-impact crafts.

achievements.title: Batch Craft Windows
achievements.description: Craft in batches between activity loops to reduce downtime.

achievements.title: Role-first Crafting
achievements.description: Prioritize recipes that directly improve your active class role.

achievements.title: Upgrade Sequencing
achievements.description: Chain recipes so each craft unlocks or improves the next tier.

achievements.title: Session-end Conversion
achievements.description: Convert farmed materials into upgrades before ending each run.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Farever Gear Items and Rarity Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Item decisions are easier when you evaluate slot impact, rarity value, and whether each piece supports your current role.

faqs.question: What gear slots should I prioritize first?
faqs.answer: Start with slots that directly impact survivability and core damage uptime for your role.

faqs.question: How should I treat rarity early on?
faqs.answer: Use rarity as a hint, then confirm whether stats and effects match your build before investing.

faqs.question: When should I replace a lower-rarity favorite?
faqs.answer: Replace it once a new item improves your real combat loop instead of only raising item level.

faqs.question: Should I keep off-role gear?
faqs.answer: Keep a small reserve for role swaps, but avoid filling storage with speculative pieces.

faqs.question: What is the best salvage rule?
faqs.answer: Salvage duplicates and low-impact gear after locking your core combat set and utility backups.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Farever Mounts Pets and Gliders
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Mobility companions and traversal collectibles improve route speed, exploration safety, and overall session efficiency.

faqs.question: How do mounts improve progression?
faqs.answer: They shorten travel time between activity hubs and reduce downtime across long exploration loops.

faqs.question: What is the practical value of pets?
faqs.answer: Pets are primarily collectible flavor and can also improve session flow through utility-oriented support effects.

faqs.question: When should I chase glider upgrades?
faqs.answer: Prioritize gliders once your core combat baseline is stable and map traversal becomes your main bottleneck.

faqs.question: Are these systems only cosmetic?
faqs.answer: Many rewards are cosmetic, while select options also influence travel comfort and route efficiency.

faqs.question: What is a balanced collection strategy?
faqs.answer: Focus first on mobility gains that help your daily routes, then pursue cosmetic completion goals.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Farever Solo and Co-op Multiplayer Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Farever supports both independent progression and party-based play. Choose the mode that matches your time budget and combat goals.

settings.name: Solo Progression
settings.description: Great for controlled pacing, focused build tuning, and uninterrupted route optimization.

settings.name: Co-op Party Runs
settings.description: Best for faster dungeon clears, role specialization, and shared learning of difficult mechanics.

settings.name: Server Region Selection
settings.description: Coordinate region choice with friends before sessions to keep latency predictable for action combat.

settings.name: Role Assignment
settings.description: Assign frontline, support, and damage responsibilities before entering difficult instances.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Farever Roadmap and Upcoming Content
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Early Access roadmap momentum matters for planning long-term builds, job investments, and group progression goals.

entries.title: Current Early Access Foundation
entries.description: Focus remains on launch stability, class balance passes, and core progression tuning.

entries.title: Upcoming Content Expansion
entries.description: Future updates target additional areas, encounter depth, and broader progression pathways.

entries.title: System Evolution and Community Feedback
entries.description: Roadmap priorities continue to evolve through active player feedback and live gameplay data.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Farever System Requirements and Performance Guide
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use this quick process to confirm compatibility, improve frame stability, and reduce launch or session disruptions.

steps.title: Validate minimum and recommended specs
steps.description: Compare your CPU, GPU, RAM, and storage against Steam requirements before performance tuning.

steps.title: Update graphics and platform drivers
steps.description: Current drivers improve shader handling and reduce common rendering or stutter issues.

steps.title: Optimize in-game graphics settings
steps.description: Lower high-impact settings first, then raise individual options gradually after stable performance.

steps.title: Pick the nearest server region
steps.description: Latency directly impacts combat responsiveness, so region selection matters as much as local frame rate.

steps.title: Re-test after each major patch
steps.description: Patch-level changes can alter performance behavior, so repeat a short benchmark run after updates.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Key answers for launch, progression, and multiplayer planning.

questions.question: Is Farever available now?
questions.answer: Yes, it is available on Steam Early Access for PC.

questions.question: What class should first-time players start with?
questions.answer: Warrior is the most forgiving opening choice, while Cleric is excellent for group-focused starts.

questions.question: Can I play solo and still progress smoothly?
questions.answer: Yes. Solo progression works well when you keep upgrades targeted and route your activities efficiently.

questions.question: How important is crafting early?
questions.answer: Very important. Jobs and recipes create steady power growth between dungeon attempts.

questions.question: What should I focus on after the first dungeon clears?
questions.answer: Refine one build path, improve weapon synergy, and use loot routing to unlock your next upgrade threshold.

questions.question: How can I keep performance stable?
questions.answer: Keep drivers updated, tune high-impact graphics settings first, and use the nearest server region for lower latency.
